metadata.description: A Go module for interacting with gh and the GitHub API from the command line.

upstream.readmeExcerpt:
# Go library for the GitHub CLI

`go-gh` is a collection of Go modules to make authoring GitHub CLI extensions easier.

Modules from this library will obey GitHub CLI conventions by default.

manifestFiles.excerpt:
module github.com/cli/go-gh/v2

go 1.25.0

require (
	github.com/AlecAivazis/survey/v2 v2.3.7
	github.com/MakeNowJust/heredoc v1.0.0
	github.com/Masterminds/sprig/v3 v3.3.0
)

analysis.summary: `cli/go-gh` is an active Go library for building command-line tooling around `gh` and the GitHub API, especially GitHub CLI extensions.
capabilities: Executes `gh` commands from Go and reads their output.
Provides default REST API client support for GitHub API access.
Respects `gh`-style environment and auth conventions such as `GH_REPO`, `GH_TOKEN`, and `GH_HOST`.
targetUsers: Go developers authoring GitHub CLI extensions.
CLI developers who want GitHub API access that follows `gh` behavior by default.
architectureNotes: Top-level layout is small and library-oriented, with core files plus `internal` and `pkg` directories.
evidence: Repository description: "A Go module for interacting with gh and the GitHub API from the command line."
README: "`go-gh` is a collection of Go modules to make authoring GitHub CLI extensions easier."

metadata.description: A Go module for interacting with gh and the GitHub API from the command line.
